title: Red Sox get good news on Roman Anthony, Garrett Crochet’s injuries
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Roman Anthony is nearing return from a hand injury, taking 12-15 pain-free swings in practice.
Garrett Crochet threw a successful live batting practice, showing improved shoulder condition with mid-90s velocity.
Both players are progressing, but no set return dates; their recovery is important for the Red Sox lineup and rotation.

title: Mass. police respond after ‘body’ reported in truck, turns out to be a mannequin
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Wakefield police responded to a report of a body in a truck, which turned out to be a mannequin.
A concerned 911 caller mistakenly identified a fire and rescue training dummy as a dead body.
Police confirmed the incident after locating the truck and finding the mannequin inside.

title: Man spent hours between wall and elevator shaft at Fields Corner MBTA station, police say
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A man broke into a secure area at Fields Corner MBTA station in Dorchester on Saturday, police stated.
The individual claimed to be homeless and stuck between the elevator shaft and wall for several hours.
Authorities plan to charge the man with trespassing following his removal by firefighters at 3:30 p.m.

title: 2-year-old bitten by raccoon at Mass. town beach
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Two-year-old Grayson Cohen was bitten by a raccoon at Wayland Town Beach while playing on a jungle gym.
Grayson was treated at Newton-Wellesley Hospital with stitches and a rabies vaccination. He has since recovered.
Wayland Police continue searching for the raccoon and issued wildlife safety precautions for the public.

title: 3 Boston cheese shops perfect for the ultimate summer picnic spread
author: Kelly Egan
bullets: American Provisions in South Boston offers artisan cheeses, charcuterie boards, and wines for summer picnics, located near M Street Beach.
Bacco's Wine and Cheese in Back Bay features global cheese selections, including a popular pre-made picnic box, near Boston Public Garden.
Crane River Cheese Club in Beacon Hill focuses on New England cheeses, offering "Cheese Club Kits," near Faneuil Hall Marketplace.

title: Boston to scale back Open Newbury Street events this year, Wu says
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Mayor Wu announces fewer Open Newbury Street events in 2026 due to a busy schedule of major city events.
Boston plans fewer Open Streets events in Jamaica Plain, Allston/Brighton, Mattapan, and Roslindale compared to last year.
Dates for Open Newbury Street, usually held on 10 Sundays and one winter day, remain unannounced for 2026.

title: Millionaires tax surges past expectations, once again
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Massachusetts' millionaires tax has generated over $3.1 billion this fiscal year, surpassing the initial $2 billion estimate.
The 4% surtax on incomes over $1 million funds transportation and public education, exceeding projected revenues for three consecutive years.
Despite revenue gains, IRS data showed a loss of over 30,000 residents in 2023, primarily impacting high-income taxpayers.

title: Someone just won a $1M scratch ticket prize in Somerville
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A Quincy trust claimed a $1 million lottery prize from a scratch ticket bought in Somerville.
The Summers Coming Trust received a $650,000 cash option from the Massachusetts State Lottery.
Holland Convenience in Somerville, where the ticket was purchased, will receive a $10,000 bonus.

title: Winthrop man pleads not guilty in wife’s shooting death
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Sean Brewer, 58, pleaded not guilty to manslaughter in the shooting death of his wife, Jacklyn Berry, in Winthrop.
Brewer was charged with illegal firearm possession and held on $100,000 bail after his court appearance in East Boston.
Berry allegedly identified Brewer as the shooter but described it as an accident; Brewer claimed the gun discharged accidentally.

title: Trillium opens new waterfront beer garden at Boston Harbor Hotel
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Trillium Brewing Company opens a new seasonal beer garden at Boston Harbor Hotel with waterfront views.
The beer garden operates Tuesday to Saturday, offering drinks, food, and live music by the water.
Location features a busy events calendar including the World Cup, Sail Boston, and Summer in the City concerts.
